content: We can frame zero-shot prompting as an implicit, emergent Neural Turing Machine where the large language model’s pre-trained parameter space acts as the NTM’s programmable external memory, allowing zero-shot queries to trigger the inference of implicit algorithms (like logical deduction or structured translation) without explicit example fine-tuning, just as NTMs infer simple algorithms from limited input-output demonstrations. This perspective reframes zero-shot generalization not as just emergent pattern matching, but as the model dynamically retrieving and executing implicit algorithmic routines stored in its broad pre-trained memory.
